use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

enum ServiceState {
    Disconnected,
    Connected { db: DbConnection },
    Ready { db: DbConnection, cache: CacheHandle },
}

struct Service {
    state: Arc<RwLock<ServiceState>>,
}

impl Service {
    fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ServiceState::Disconnected)),
        }
    }

    async fn connect(&self) {
        let conn = DbConnection::connect("localhost");
        let mut state = self.state.write().await;
        *state = ServiceState::Connected { db: conn };
    }

    async fn is_ready(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(&*self.state.read().await, ServiceState::Ready { .. })
    }

    async fn query(&self, sql: &str) -> Option<String> {
        let state = self.state.read().await;
        match &*state {
            ServiceState::Ready { db, .. } => Some(db.execute(sql)),
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}

struct DbConnection;
impl DbConnection {
    fn connect(_addr: &str) -> Self { Self }
    fn execute(&self, _sql: &str) -> String { todo!() }
}

struct CacheHandle;
